1: Now in the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, that the word of the LORD LORD by the mouth of Jeremiah might be fulfilled, the LORD LORD stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia, that he made a proclamation throughout all his kingdom, and put it put it also in writing, saying,
2: Thus saith Cyrus king of Persia, The LORD LORD God of heaven hath given me all the kingdoms of the earth; and he hath charged me to build him an house at Jerusalem, which is is in Judah.
3: Who is there is there among you of all his people? his God be with him, and let him go up to Jerusalem, which is is in Judah, and build the house of the LORD LORD God of Israel, (he is is the God,) which is is in Jerusalem.
4: And whosoever remaineth in any place where he sojourneth, let the men of his place help him with silver, and with gold, and with goods, and with beasts, beside the freewill offering for the house of God that is is in Jerusalem.
5: Then rose up the chief of the fathers of Judah and Benjamin, and the priests, and the Levites, with all them them whose spirit God had raised, to go up to build the house of the LORD LORD which is is in Jerusalem.
6: And all they that were were about them strengthened their hands with vessels of silver, with gold, with goods, and with beasts, and with precious things, beside all that that was willingly offered.
7: Also Cyrus the king brought forth the vessels of the house of the LORD LORD , which Nebuchadnezzar had brought forth out of Jerusalem, and had put them in the house of his gods;
8: even those did Cyrus king of Persia bring forth by the hand of Mithredath the treasurer, and numbered them unto Sheshbazzar, the prince of Judah.
9: And this is is the number of them: thirty chargers of gold, a thousand chargers of silver, nine and twenty knives,
10: thirty basons of gold, silver basons of a second sort sort four hundred and ten, and and other vessels a thousand.
11: All the vessels of gold and of silver were were five thousand and four hundred. All these these did Sheshbazzar bring up with them of them of the captivity that were brought up from Babylon unto Jerusalem.
